target: Text patches: SEC-4.2.2 TLDR wording precision, WIN-026 rhetorical drag + closing sentence clarity, SEC-6.14 storm-corollary citation hedge

patches.description: ISS-1715: TLDR for SEC-4.2.2 says '3.6× outside its ±3 nT window' which is ambiguous (does 'outside' modify '3.6×' or is '3.6×' the scalar?). Body text already says '3.6× the rule's ±3 nT half-width'. TLDR updated to match — 'the half-width' is precise and consistent with the body calculation |−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6.

patches.find_pattern: Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point — the actual empirical content of the WIN — is never tested by the script; it cannot be
patches.replace_pattern: The actual empirical content of the WIN — anticrepuscular ray convergence at the anti-solar point — is not validated by the script at all; it cannot be
patches.description: ISS-1792: Rearranges the sentence to lead with 'The actual empirical content' (clearer subject) and replaces 'is never tested' with 'is not validated...at all' (removes latent hedging tone of 'never'). Applies to WIN-026 detail_verdict_text.

patches.find_pattern: can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source.
patches.replace_pattern: can manufacture parallel rays from a finite-altitude source.
patches.description: ISS-1799: 'finite source' lacks context for readers unfamiliar with the discussion — a photon source at finite altitude, not just any finite physical source. Adding 'altitude' resolves the ambiguity. Applies to WIN-026 detail_evidence text. Note: 'any finite source' in the same paragraph remains unchanged (that instance is preceded by 'geometrically incompatible with' and is self-explanatory in context).

patches.find_pattern: dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them.
patches.replace_pattern: no published storm-period SG coupling survey has appeared in the accessible literature.
patches.description: ISS-1966: The phrase 'dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them' asserts the existence of systematic searches without citing one. The replacement hedges accurately: the absence is noted as absence from the accessible literature, not as the result of a dedicated survey we have completed. This is the correct epistemic claim given we have not done a systematic storm-by-storm SG residual sweep (as acknowledged two sentences later: 'WIN-058b's verdict remains Not Demonstrated pending a focused storm-by-storm survey'). The Van Camp et al. (2001) and Sun et al. (2010) citations already in the section cover eclipse-period SG nulls; storm-period coupling is a distinct claim requiring its own evidence.
